findings: Teardown sends single SIGTERM per pid with no wait/escalation/process-group kill, risking orphaned child processes.
--teardown executes before required --repo validation, so run dir discovery can fail and pid cleanup can be skipped.
PID capture relies on fixed sleep plus leaf-process name heuristics; custom/slow agent commands can produce stale or missing pid files.
Relaunch kills tmux session but does not pre-clear stale pid files, risking status/teardown actions on outdated PIDs.
